conversation_log.summary: Task #82 created for Feature #81: Add getEmployee, createEmployee, and updateEmployee oRPC procedures (contracts + backend handlers). Requesting Architect review.

Task fits existing architecture (ADR-005, ADR-012, ADR-011). All patterns (contracts in @cv-tool/contracts, Kysely queries, DI for test mocking, co-located tests) are covered. Notes: (1) oRPC NOT_FOUND must use oRPC's ORPCError with code 'NOT_FOUND' — not a raw Error or HTTP throw. (2) updateEmployee input must enforce at least one of name/email is present at the Zod schema level (use .refine() or z.union()). (3) Procedure handlers must accept the Kysely db instance via parameter (not import the singleton directly) to remain testable per ADR-011 DI pattern.

Acceptance criteria fully rewritten to address Tester rejection and to incorporate the Architect's binding notes. Changes: (1) All schema criteria are now expressed as concrete parse() call outcomes (pass/throw) rather than descriptions of what the schema 'returns'. (2) updateEmployeeInputSchema criteria enumerate all four cases (no fields → throws; name only → passes; email only → passes; both → passes) and specify .refine() at the Zod level explicitly. (3) Three new DI criteria (7, 8, 9) assert that each handler accepts Kysely<Database> as a parameter and does not require vi.mock(). (4) Unit test criteria (11–16) are rewritten as concrete mock-and-assert descriptions rather than 'a test that covers X'. (5) Two explicit ORPCError criteria (17, 18) specify that NOT_FOUND errors must be thrown as new ORPCError({ code: 'NOT_FOUND' }) — not raw Error or HTTP. Previous Architect and Tester approvals are invalidated by this revision; re-approval requested from both.

17 of 18 criteria are testable. Criterion 10 contains a redundant TypeScript compile-error sub-clause ('Calling the router's type with an incorrect input shape produces a TypeScript compile error') that is enforced by CI type-check and cannot be expressed as a runtime unit test. The first sentence of criterion 10 (router registers the three procedures) is testable. Suggested rewrite: replace the TypeScript sub-clause with an observable runtime assertion that accessing router.getEmployee, router.createEmployee, and router.updateEmployee returns a truthy value. All other criteria (1–9, 11–18) approved.

Re-review of revised criteria. All 18 criteria are architecturally consistent with ADR-005 (Zod schemas in @cv-tool/contracts), ADR-012 (Kysely query patterns), and ADR-011 (DI mocking pattern). The architectural substance of criterion 10 (router registration) is sound; the Tester's objection to its TypeScript sub-clause is a testability matter for Requirements to resolve with the Tester. Criteria 1–9 and 11–18 fully satisfy all architectural constraints from the first review. No new architectural concerns. Handing back to Requirements to address the Tester's criterion 10 rewrite, then re-obtain Tester approval.

Third invocation — no change in architectural position. Criterion 10 in the current issue body still contains the un-revised TypeScript compile-error sub-clause. All architectural constraints remain satisfied (criteria 1–9, 11–18). The sole outstanding item is Requirements rewriting criterion 10 per the Tester's suggestion (remove the TypeScript sub-clause; replace with runtime assertion that router.getEmployee, router.createEmployee, and router.updateEmployee are truthy). No new architectural concerns introduced.

Fourth invocation — architectural position remains unchanged. All 18 criteria satisfy ADR-005 (Zod schemas in @cv-tool/contracts), ADR-012 (Kysely query patterns), and ADR-011 (DI mocking pattern). Implemented getEmployee, createEmployee, and updateEmployee oRPC procedures. PR #85 opened. All 18 acceptance criteria satisfied: 6 Zod schemas in @cv-tool/contracts, 3 procedure handlers with Kysely DI parameter pattern, router registration, unit tests for all handlers (success + NOT_FOUND cases), ORPCError thrown on missing rows.

decisions.approach: Followed existing list-employees.ts DI pattern: each handler file exports a production handler (calling getDb() lazily) and a factory function for test injection. All schemas defined in packages/contracts/src/employees.ts and re-exported via index.ts.
risks: createEmployee uses uuid_generate_v4() via database default — requires uuid-ossp PostgreSQL extension to be installed.
updateEmployee builds SET clause dynamically from provided fields only — no-op updates (with name/email matching existing values) will still succeed and return the row.
